source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure 'cross db ownership chaining' Database Flag for Cloud SQL SQL Server Instance Is Set to 'off'

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure 'cross db ownership chaining' Database Flag for Cloud SQL SQL Server Instance Is Set to 'off'
input_row.description: It is recommended to set cross db ownership chaining database flag for Cloud SQL SQL Server instance to off. This flag is deprecated for all SQL Server versions in CGP. Going forward, you can't set its value to on. However, if you have this flag enabled, we strongly recommend that you either remove the flag from your database or set it to off. For cross-database access, use the Microsoft tutorial for signing stored procedures with a certificate.
input_row.rationale: Use the cross db ownership for chaining option to configure cross-database ownership chaining for an instance of Microsoft SQL Server. This server option allows you to control cross-database ownership chaining at the database level or to allow cross-database ownership chaining for all databases. Enabling cross db ownership is not recommended unless all of the databases hosted by the instance of SQL Server must participate in cross-database ownership chaining and you are aware of the security implications of this setting. This recommendation is applicable to SQL Server database instances. Impact: Updating flags may cause the database to restart. This may cause it to unavailable for a short amount of time, so this is best done at a time of low usage. You should also determine if the tables in your databases reference another table without using credentials for that database, as turning off cross database ownership will break this relationship.
input_row.audit: NOTE: This flag is deprecated for all SQL Server versions. Going forward, you can't set its value to on. However, if you have this flag enabled it should be removed from your database or set to off. From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console. 2. Select the instance to open its Instance Overview page 3. Ensure the database flag cross db ownership chaining that has been set is listed under the Database flags section. From Google Cloud CLI 1. Ensure the below command returns off for every Cloud SQL SQL Server database instance: gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> --format=json | jq '.settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="cross db ownership chaining")|.value' In the output, database flags are listed under the settings as the collection databaseFlags.
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances. 2. Select the SQL Server instance for which you want to enable to database flag. 3. Click Edit. 4. Scroll down to the Flags section. 5. To set a flag that has not been set on the instance before, click Add item, choose the flag cross db ownership chaining from the drop-down menu, and set its value to off. 6. Click Save. 7. Confirm the changes under Flags on the Overview page. From Google Cloud CLI 1. Configure the cross db ownership chaining database flag for every Cloud SQL SQL Server database instance using the below command: gcloud sql instances patch <INSTANCE_NAME> --database-flags "cross db ownership chaining"=off Note : This command will overwrite all database flags previously set. To keep those and add new ones, include the values for all flags to be set on the instance; any flag not specifically included is set to its default value. For flags that do not take a value, specify the flag name followed by an equals sign ("="). Default Value: This flag is deprecated for all SQL Server versions. Going forward, you can't set its value to on. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/sqlserver/flags 2. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/database-engine/configure-windows/cross- db-ownership-chaining-server-configuration-option?view=sql-server-ver15 Additional Information: WARNING: This patch modifies database flag values, which may require your instance to be restarted. Check the list of supported flags - https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/sqlserver/flags - to see if your instance will be restarted when this patch is submitted. Note : Some database flag settings can affect instance availability or stability, and remove the instance from the Cloud SQL SLA. For information about these flags, see Operational Guidelines. Note : Configuring the above flag does not restart the Cloud SQL instance.
input_row.impact: Updating flags may cause the database to restart. This may cause it to unavailable for a short amount of time, so this is best done at a time of low usage. You should also determine if the tables in your databases reference another table without using credentials for that database, as turning off cross database ownership will break this relationship.
input_row.references: 1. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/sqlserver/flags 2. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/database-engine/configure-windows/cross- db-ownership-chaining-server-configuration-option?view=sql-server-ver15 Additional Information: WARNING: This patch modifies database flag values, which may require your instance to be restarted. Check the list of supported flags - https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/sqlserver/flags - to see if your instance will be restarted when this patch is submitted. Note : Some database flag settings can affect instance availability or stability, and remove the instance from the Cloud SQL SLA. For information about these flags, see Operational Guidelines. Note : Configuring the above flag does not restart the Cloud SQL instance.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control requires checking the configuration of a database flag for Cloud SQL SQL Server instances, specifically the 'cross db ownership chaining' flag. Given the fields provided and my knowledge of GCP services, this check can be conducted using GCP APIs and tools like Google Cloud SDK (gcloud), which allow for querying Cloud SQL instance configurations.

The audit field suggests using the `gcloud sql instances describe` command to verify the value of the 'cross db ownership chaining' flag, which indicates that this property is accessible through GCP's command-line interface. This aligns with the criteria for an automated check, as GCP APIs support accessing and evaluating database flag settings.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_sql_instance_cross_db_ownership_chaining_off

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) `sql instances describe` command to retrieve the configuration of Cloud SQL instances.
- Specifically, check the `settings.databaseFlags` array for any entries with `name` equal to "cross db ownership chaining".
- Validate that the `value` for "cross db ownership chaining" is set to "off" for each SQL Server instance.
- Enumerate all Cloud SQL instances across the relevant project(s) to ensure completeness of the check.
- Pass Criteria: All relevant Cloud SQL SQL Server instances have the 'cross db ownership chaining' flag set to "off".
- Fail Criteria: Any instance is found with the 'cross db ownership chaining' flag set to a value other than "off".
- Example command: `gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> --format=json | jq '.settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="cross db ownership chaining")|.value'` to verify the flag value is "off".
step2_review:
---

### 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: **AGREE**

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:**  
  The classification as "Automated" is correct. The control requires checking a Cloud SQL database flag, which is directly accessible via GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud). There is no need for manual inspection or console-based review.
- **GCP API/SDK/CLI Capability:**  
  The `gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME>` command returns all instance settings, including `databaseFlags`. This can be programmatically queried and parsed for compliance checks.
- **Other GCP Services:**  
  While GCP Config (now part of Config Controller) does not natively support deep introspection of Cloud SQL flags, the gcloud CLI and REST API are sufficient and standard for this check.

---

### 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: **Score: 9/10**

- **Automation Details:**  
  The described automation is technically correct. The use of `gcloud sql instances describe` is appropriate, and parsing `settings.databaseFlags` for the relevant flag is the right approach.
- **gcloud Command:**  
  The example command is valid, assuming `jq` is available for JSON parsing. However, for full automation across all instances, a loop or bulk listing (`gcloud sql instances list`) should be included to enumerate all instances.
- **Manual Steps:**  
  Not applicable, as the assessment is automated.
- **Program Name:**  
  The program name `gcp_sql_instance_cross_db_ownership_chaining_off` is clear and descriptive, but could be improved for precision and consistency (see Improvements below).

---

### 3. IMPROVEMENTS

- **Bulk Enumeration:**  
  The assessment should explicitly mention enumerating all Cloud SQL instances in the project(s), not just checking a single instance. This can be achieved via:
  ```bash
  for INSTANCE in $(gcloud sql instances list --format="value(name)"); do
    gcloud sql instances describe $INSTANCE --format=json | \
      jq '.settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="cross db ownership chaining") | .value'
  done
  ```
- **Instance Type Filtering:**  
  The check should ensure that only SQL Server instances are evaluated, as the flag is only relevant for SQL Server. This can be done by filtering on `.databaseVersion` (e.g., `SQLSERVER_2017_STANDARD`).
- **API Alternative:**  
  The Cloud SQL Admin API (`projects.instances.list` and `projects.instances.get`) can also be used for automation, especially for integration with third-party compliance tools.
- **Program Name Suggestion:**  
  For clarity and convention, consider:  
  `gcp_sql_sqlserver_instance_cross_db_ownership_chaining_off`
  - This makes it explicit that the check is for SQL Server instances, not all Cloud SQL types.
- **Edge Case:**  
  If the flag is not present at all (since "on" is deprecated), clarify whether this is compliant (it should be).

---

### 4. CONFIDENCE: **HIGH**

- The assessment is technically sound, leverages the correct GCP APIs/SDK, and aligns with GCP security best practices for automation.

---

### 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: **Modify**

- **Modify** the automation details to:
  - Explicitly enumerate all Cloud SQL instances in the project(s).
  - Filter only SQL Server instances.
  - Clarify that absence of the flag is compliant (since "on" is deprecated and "off" is default).
  - Optionally, update the program name to `gcp_sql_sqlserver_instance_cross_db_ownership_chaining_off` for clarity and convention.

---

**Summary Table:**

| Section             | Assessment                          |
|---------------------|-------------------------------------|
| Approach Validation | AGREE                               |
| Technical Accuracy  | 9/10                                |
| Improvements        | Bulk enumeration, filter by engine, clarify flag absence, program name |
| Confidence          | HIGH                                |
| Final Recommendation| Modify (see above)                  |

---
